discovery.run_summary: Quiet tape overall with SPY flat, but a sharp internal rotation: semis (SMH -1.83%) and semicap equipment names sold off hard while energy (XLE +1.87%), staples, financials, and defense caught a bid amid ongoing Iran situation. The standout pattern is a coordinated semicap drawdown (ONTO, ENTG, AEIS, VECO, PLAB all down 4-6% on the day, double-digit five-day losses) likely tied to the Seagate-led memory sell-off and broader AI-capex anxiety into NVDA earnings.

notable_index_moves: SMH -1.83% and XBI -2.31% leading downside; semicap equipment cluster down ~5% on the day
XLE +1.87% on Iran/Strait of Hormuz tension; Trump postponed strike
VIX -3.3% to ~17.8 despite tech weakness — no broad fear, just sector-specific
ORCL -3.3%, TSLA -2.9%, TSM -2.1% — AI/mega-cap tech under pressure into NVDA print

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries
discoveries.setup: Semicap-adjacent name down with the group, but today's 8-K is a financing/securities transaction not an operational warning
discoveries.thesis: AEIS filed an 8-K today with items 1.01 (material agreement), 2.03 (financial obligation creation), and 3.02 (unregistered equity sale) — this signature is a convertible-note or financing deal, not a guide cut. The stock is being lumped in with the broader semicap selloff (ONTO, ENTG, VECO all down similarly) on the back of the Seagate memory-capex commentary, but the company-specific catalyst is a capital-structure event whose dilution/coupon terms could easily be priced in or even bullish if proceeds fund accretive use. The five-day drawdown is steep relative to sector peers and likely overshoots if the financing terms are reasonable.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reading the 8-K shows convert pricing at a healthy premium with use of proceeds for buyback or acquisition; no change to forward guidance; peer semicap names stabilize tomorrow
discoveries.what_kills: 8-K reveals deeply dilutive equity issuance at a discount, or proceeds earmarked to cover an operational shortfall; semicap selloff intensifies on further negative AI-capex commentary
discoveries.what_to_learn: 8-K item combinations 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02 filed together almost always indicate a convertible or structured financing — the market initially treats this as bad news but terms determine whether the reaction is justified; reading the actual exhibit before fading is mandatory
discoveries.catalyst: Today's 8-K with financing signature (1.01/2.03/3.02) on top of ongoing semicap sector pressure
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: The item combo 1.01 + 2.03 + 3.02 is the classic convertible-debt issuance fingerprint; this is a capital-structure event being conflated with the semicap macro selloff
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibits to identify convert size, coupon, conversion premium, and use of proceeds
Check if the 5/13 and 5/14 8-Ks were a guidance update or just investor day boilerplate
Compare AEIS five-day drawdown to peer semicap names — is the underperformance company-specific or sector beta
Map AEIS exposure to memory vs logic capex — Seagate commentary is memory-specific

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation
discoveries.setup: Semicap equipment name down with the sector group on AI-capex anxiety, with a same-day 8-K of unclear severity
discoveries.thesis: ONTO is moving with the broader semicap cohort (ENTG, VECO, AEIS, PLAB all down 4-6% today and double-digits over five days), driven by Seagate's commentary that new memory factories 'take too long' to build — which the market is reading as a near-term AI-capex pause signal. ONTO's own 8.01 8-K today is generic 'other events' without 2.02 earnings or 5.02 officer items, so it's likely a press release or routine disclosure rather than a guide cut. The magnitude of the move is consistent with sector beta plus mild company-specific news.
discoveries.what_confirms: 8-K exhibit is a routine corporate announcement (conference presentation, dividend, etc.); ONTO trades in line with semicap peers in coming sessions
discoveries.what_kills: 8-K reveals a customer loss, project cancellation, or M&A news; ONTO materially underperforms semicap peers tomorrow
discoveries.catalyst: Sector-wide semicap selloff on Seagate AI-capex commentary, plus a same-day 8.01 filing
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8.01 alone with 9.01 boilerplate is typically a press release; the move size matches sector peers without 8-Ks (ENTG, VECO), suggesting this is sector-driven
research_pointers: Read the ONTO 8-K exhibit to confirm it's routine
Track semicap basket vs SMH over next 2-3 sessions to confirm sector-beta thesis
Watch NVDA earnings reaction — bullish print could relieve the whole cohort

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security
discoveries.setup: Defense name rallying with ITA +1.36% on Iran tensions, after a routine annual-meeting 8-K
discoveries.thesis: KTOS's 5/15 8-K is item 5.07 (shareholder vote results) — annual meeting boilerplate, not a catalyst. Today's move is straightforward: ITA defense ETF +1.4%, Trump's Iran posture is escalatory (postponed strike at allies' request, with surrender rhetoric on Truth Social), and KTOS is a high-beta unmanned-systems play within defense. Magnitude is appropriate given its typical sector beta of ~2-3x ITA on geopolitical days.
discoveries.what_confirms: Iran tensions persist or escalate; ITA continues higher; KTOS beta to ITA remains in normal range
discoveries.what_kills: Iran de-escalates suddenly; defense ETF rolls over and KTOS holds bid — would suggest unseen company catalyst worth investigating
discoveries.catalyst: Iran geopolitical escalation lifting defense sector
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 8-K is 5.07 (shareholder vote) — non-material; rally is sector-driven by ITA +1.4% on Iran headlines
research_pointers: Track Iran headline flow as binary driver
Compare KTOS move to LMT, RTX, GD, AVAV as a peer-beta sanity check

discoveries.name: Veracyte
discoveries.setup: Diagnostics mid-cap up sharply on elevated volume with no 8-K and no scheduled earnings
discoveries.thesis: VCYT up nearly 8% with 1.4x volume but catalyst_signals is empty — no SEC filing, no earnings window. XBI is down 2.3% so this is not biotech sympathy; it's idiosyncratic and against the tape. Could be a sell-side upgrade, conference presentation reception, M&A rumor, or guideline-inclusion news that didn't surface in the news feed. Without visible evidence I cannot articulate either a fade or follow-through case at confidence 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first
discoveries.what_kills: n/a — need to identify the catalyst first
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — empty catalyst signals, against-tape move in healthcare diagnostics
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: No 8-K, no earnings within window, no news headlines, and direction opposes XBI -2.3% — clearly company-specific but driver not visible
research_pointers: Check sell-side rating changes today on VCYT
Search for clinical guideline inclusions, payer coverage decisions, or oncology conference presentations (ASCO is late May)
Scan for M&A chatter — strategic acquirers in molecular diagnostics include Exact, Natera, Roche

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Seagate CEO comments that new memory factories 'take too long' to build, triggering memory and semicap equipment selloff
Trump postpones Iran strike but escalates rhetoric; Strait of Hormuz remains contested

trump_signals.post_summary: Iran escalation rhetoric — taunts about surrender, alongside news Trump postponed a strike at Saudi/UAE/Qatar request
trump_signals.note: Posture remains escalatory despite the postponement; defense and energy both bid today. Risk is binary — a de-escalation headline would unwind both legs quickly.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic acquires Stainless, the SDK automation startup used by OpenAI, Google, and Cloudflare
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic acquires a dev tools/SDK automation company that serves multiple major AI labs. Niche developer infrastructure acquisition.
ai_announcements._note: No publicly traded stock in today's mover list has direct exposure to Stainless or SDK automation. The semiconductor equipment selloff (ONTO, PLAB, AEIS, VECO, ENTG) appears unrelated to this announcement — more likely sector-wide weakness tied to semi-cap concerns. No clean mechanism links the Stainless deal to today's movers.

ai_announcements.headline: Amazon Alexa+ can now generate AI podcast episodes on demand
ai_announcements.summary: Amazon expands Alexa+ into AI-generated personalized podcast content.
ai_announcements._note: Potential read-throughs to SPOT, IHRT, SIRI, podcast hosting (LBRDA), but none appear in today's mover list. No actionable signal from supplied market data.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The biggest moves today are in semiconductor equipment names (ONTO -6.3%, ENTG -4.4%, PLAB -5.0%, VECO -5.1%, AEIS -4.5%), all down ~9-15% over five days. This looks like a semi-cap sector drawdown unrelated to the supplied AI announcements (Stainless acquisition, Musk lawsuit, Alexa podcasts, AI glasses optics). Not enough linkage to call any of these AI-news-driven irrational selloffs. The Anthropic/Stainless deal is a developer-tools tuck-in with no direct public-company victim in the mover list. Quiet night for AI-news-to-stock-mover mapping.
